commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=de2e123fbc30c0d62bde30e2824e595f2dc2f8da)
commands.review_summary:
The PR post-processes Zod-generated bundled channel config JSON Schema to remove defaulted properties from `required`, regenerates bundled channel metadata, and adds Feishu/channel schema regression tests.

PR surface: Source +105, Tests +103, Generated -1. Total +207 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main validates bundled channel config with generated JSON Schema and `applyDefaults: false` while Feishu has defaulted fields that runtime Zod accepts. I did not run a packaged 2026.5.2 upgrade reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair on the PR branch: replace the new Feishu test core-internal imports with public plugin SDK subpaths and rerun targeted validation.

target.title: Feishu channel crashes after upgrading to 2026.5.2 — appId/appSecret fields incompatible
target.body:
## Summary

- Bundled channel config metadata generated by `scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts` marks Zod `.optional().default(...)` and `.default(...)` fields as JSON Schema `required`, because Zod v4's `toJSONSchema` treats "has a default value" as "always present at runtime".
- This produced 35 defaulted-required leaks across 17 channels in `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`; on Feishu alone, eight defaulted fields (`domain`, `connectionMode`, `webhookPath`, `dmPolicy`, `groupPolicy`, `reactionNotifications`, `typingIndicator`, `resolveSenderNames`) were listed as `required`, so the bundled schema rejected the very config shapes that the runtime Zod parser accepted.
- The reporter's #77116 config (omitting `domain`, `connectionMode`, `webhookPath`, `reactionNotifications`, `typingIndicator`, `resolveSenderNames`) was rejected by JSON Schema validation while the runtime Zod parser accepted it, contributing to the channel-config crash loop after the 2026.5.x upgrade.

Why now: the Codex review on #77116 confirmed that current `main` still has the generator-level bug, and tagged the issue `P1`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`, and `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`. A source-level generator repair is the narrow, root-cause-shaped next step.

Intended outcome: bundled channel config metadata stops marking defaulted fields as `required`, while the runtime Zod parser continues to supply the same defaults. The packaged gateway's JSON Schema validation accepts the same shapes the runtime parser accepts.

Out of scope: the reporter's separately installed `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` external plugin path is not touched. Stale external plugin metadata / doctor cleanup is left for maintainer follow-up, as flagged by the Codex review.

Success looks like: `node scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write` regenerates bundled metadata with zero `required` fields across all 24 channels; the reporter's config from #77116 is accepted by both the runtime Zod parser and the generated JSON Schema, with and without `applyDefaults`.

Reviewers should focus on: the `relaxDefaultedRequired` helper added to `src/channels/plugins/config-schema.ts`, the regenerated `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`, and the four new regression cases under `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts > FeishuConfigSchema #77116 regression`.

## Linked context

Closes #77116

Related: Codex review on #77116 (referenced commit `a189baa4b344`), Feishu channel schema history at `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts`.

Not requested by a maintainer; this is an external contribution targeting the source-level generator bug identified in the Codex review.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `channels.feishu` config (and the same shape on 16 other channels) was rejected by bundled JSON Schema validation because Zod v4's `toJSONSchema` marked `.optional().default(...)` and `.default(...)` fields as `required`, while the runtime Zod parser accepted the same shapes. This mismatch contributed to the gateway crash loop in #77116.
- Real environment tested: source checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on `main`, with the patch applied and bundled channel config metadata regenerated in-place via `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Regenerate bundled metadata: `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write`
  - Inspect Feishu schema's `required` and `default` keywords in the regenerated file (script-driven check below).
  - Run the regression test set covering the generator, the Feishu runtime parser, the generated JSON Schema (with and without defaults), the bundled runtime test, and the shared schema validator / defaults helpers.
- Evidence after fix (copied live output): Bundled metadata regeneration writes the new file:

```
$ node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write
[bundled-channel-config-metadata] wrote src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts
```

Bundled metadata no longer marks any Feishu property as required, while every default value is preserved:

```
=== Feishu #77116 bundled schema regression summary ===
schema.required (current/main after fix): []
defaulted property count: 8
  domain                 default="feishu"         ok (default supplies value)
  connectionMode         default="websocket"      ok (default supplies value)
  webhookPath            default="/feishu/events" ok (default supplies value)
  dmPolicy               default="pairing"        ok (default supplies value)
  groupPolicy            default="allowlist"      ok (default supplies value)
  reactionNotifications  default="own"            ok (default supplies value)
  typingIndicator        default=true             ok (default supplies value)
  resolveSenderNames     default=true             ok (default supplies value)

PASS: 0 defaulted fields leaked into required.
```

Regression test set covering generator + runtime + JSON Schema acceptance:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts \
    src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts \
    src/config/bundled-channel-config-runtime.test.ts \
    src/plugins/schema-validator.test.ts \
    src/shared/json-schema-defaults.test.ts

 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  85 passed (85)
```

Exit code: 0

- Observed result after fix: validation passes with zero `required` fields across all 24 bundled channels (previously 35 defaulted-required leaks across 17 channels); the reporter's #77116 config is accepted by both the runtime Zod parser and the generated JSON Schema, with and without `applyDefaults: true`.
- What was not tested: a live packaged upgrade from 2026.4.x to a build containing this patch was not run in this environment (no npm packaged build available locally); runtime telemetry / live gateway crash-loop instrumentation was not added.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: validation is source-level (Vitest on the worktree) and bundled-metadata regeneration against the same source tree. The reporter's environment additionally used the external `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` plugin, which is intentionally out of scope; the bundled schema path that this PR repairs is the path the Codex review identified as still broken on `main`.
- Before evidence: the previously generated `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` listed `schema.required` as `["domain","connectionMode","webhookPath","dmPolicy","groupPolicy","reactionNotifications","typingIndicator","resolveSenderNames"]` for Feishu, while every one of those eight fields carried a `default` keyword in `properties`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write` (regenerate bundled metadata after the generator fix).
- `pnpm config set verify-deps-before-run false` (workaround so `pnpm exec` does not retrigger install in this worktree; not part of the patch).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/schema-validator.test.ts src/shared/json-schema-defaults.test.ts` — 85 / 85 passing.

Regression coverage added:

- `src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts`:
  - `buildChannelConfigSchema > does not mark fields with defaults as required (Feishu #77116 regression)` — asserts that `.default(...)` and `.optional().default(...)` fields are dropped from `required` while non-defaulted fields stay required.
  - `buildChannelConfigSchema > keeps defaulted fields reachable as properties and as runtime defaults` — asserts that properties still carry `default` and `safeParse({})` still returns the default value.
- `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts > FeishuConfigSchema #77116 regression (appId/appSecret/defaulted-required)` — four cases:
  - reporter config from #77116 is accepted by `FeishuConfigSchema.safeParse`,
  - `buildChannelConfigSchema(FeishuConfigSchema).schema.required` is `[]`,
  - reporter config is accepted by `validateJsonSchemaValue` with `applyDefaults: true`,
  - reporter config is accepted by `validateJsonSchemaValue` with `applyDefaults: false`.

Extension-specific plugin contract test: the Feishu extension test suite runs the shared schema generator against the extension's Zod schema, and asserts the generator output matches the runtime parser contract for the #77116 reporter config (valid extension id, valid account schemas, valid default application paths). This is the extension test that catches any future regression of the bundled-schema acceptance for Feishu.

What failed before this fix: the new `does not mark fields with defaults as required (Feishu #77116 regression)` test failed before implementation with `expected [ 'alwaysRequired', 'withDefault', 'optionalWithDefault' ] to deeply equal [ 'alwaysRequired' ]`, confirming the bug shape before the fix was applied.

The pre-existing `buildChannelConfigSchema > builds json schema when toJSONSchema is available` assertion was updated to drop `required: ["enabled"]`, because that test enshrined the exact behavior this PR fixes.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No` for end users; `Yes` for tools that consume the bundled channel config metadata as JSON Schema (they now see fewer `required` constraints, matching the runtime parser contract).

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` — bundled JSON Schema is regenerated; `required` arrays shrink or disappear for channels that previously listed defaulted fields. No config migration is required from end users: any config the runtime parser already accepts is now also accepted by the bundled JSON Schema.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: external tooling (IDE extensions, doctor checks, packaging tools) that relied on the bundled JSON Schema's `required` arrays to mean "must be present in user config". Such tooling will now correctly treat defaulted fields as optional, which matches the runtime Zod parser's behavior; no field that lacked a default loses its `required` marker.

How is that risk mitigated? The helper is recursive and only drops a property from `required` when that same property carries a `default` keyword in the same schema object, so non-defaulted required fields stay required everywhere. Regression coverage includes the `alwaysRequired` non-defaulted field staying in `required`, plus the runtime default-application behavior is preserved by the unchanged `safeParse` path.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Await maintainer review on the generator-level scope (one shared helper, regenerated bundled metadata, no channel-specific shims).

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on the author side — local validation, bundled-metadata regeneration, and regression coverage are all in place. Awaiting maintainer review and CI verification once the PR opens.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? The Codex review on #77116 explicitly identified the generator-level input-schema repair as the next step; this PR implements that repair and regenerates the bundled metadata in the same commit.

- Fix classification: root-cause generator-level fix (shared schema helper, no channel-specific shims). The fix targets the `buildChannelConfigSchema` helper that generates JSON Schema from the Zod parser for every bundled channel; the upstream root is in the schema normalization layer, not in any individual channel's runtime behavior.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high. The fix is local, recursive, fully covered by regression tests, and the regenerated bundled metadata is verifiable by anyone running the generator script.
- Root cause: Zod v4's `toJSONSchema` marks `.optional().default(...)` and `.default(...)` fields as JSON Schema `required`, because Zod treats "has a default value" as "always present at runtime". The bundled metadata generator passed that output straight through, so 35 defaulted fields across 17 channels (8 on Feishu) leaked into `required` while the runtime Zod parser still applied the same defaults. This is a generator-level schema contract mismatch, not a per-channel bug.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the repair lands in the shared `buildChannelConfigSchema` helper used by every bundled channel, so all 24 channels benefit from one change instead of needing per-channel shims. The bundled schema now matches the runtime parser contract because the source of the divergence (Zod `toJSONSchema` semantics) is post-processed by the helper, and the regenerated metadata is checked in alongside the helper so the contract stays consistent.
- What did NOT change: per-channel schemas (`extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts` and the other 23 channels' Zod schemas) are unchanged — only the shared generator and its regenerated output change. No migration, no config breaking change, no public API change; the only behavior change is that the bundled JSON Schema stops rejecting config shapes the runtime parser already accepts. Out of scope: the external `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` plugin path, doctor cleanup, and any non-bundled-schema packaging follow-up.

target.title: Add manual provider subscription reminder foundation
target.body:
Narrow, manual-only foundation PR for provider subscription reminders.

Adds:
- Provider-neutral manual subscription snapshot model
- Optional local config fields for manual renewal/expiry dates
- Separate subscription menu line formatting (not quota/reset)
- Dedupeable reminder timing logic (30/7/3/1 days, today, expired)
- Focused tests

Does not add:
- Billing-page scraping, cookie/session reads, or billing endpoint calls
- Any diagnostic export changes
- Any raw account/billing identifiers or raw provider payload storage
target.author: yuxin-qiao
